mail_settings.title: Outbound mail (panel notifications)
mail_settings.subtitle: Password resets and system mail. Manage here without SSH or editing .env.
mail_settings.hint: The install script defaults to local Postfix + sendmail. Choose SMTP for SendGrid, corporate relay, etc.
mail_settings.password_unchanged_hint: Leave empty to keep the current password
mail_settings.test_hint: Test uses saved settings; click Save first if you changed the form.

stack.title: Server packages (PHP & mail)
stack.subtitle: Whitelisted bundles the engine can install via sudo on Debian/Ubuntu.
stack.warning: This runs apt installs; use backups and a maintenance window in production. After Postfix/Dovecot you still need DNS, firewall, and app (.env SMTP) configuration.
stack.empty_hint: If the list is empty, the engine may be down or the API key may not match.

terminal.title: SSH terminal
terminal.subtitle: Shell as the same user as the engine (www-data). Requires Nginx /engine-ws/ proxy and ENGINE_API_SECRET matching engine jwt_secret.
terminal.warning: This is not full root access; use carefully. Per-customer jailed shells may be added later.

server_setup.subtitle: What is ready in production and what must be enabled via .env or the OS.
server_setup.one_click_note: Installing MySQL, Postfix, or an authoritative DNS stack from the browser is not safe or portable; use Ansible, Docker, or your image. This page is a status dashboard and checklist (e.g. WordPress).
server_setup.web_hint: Adding a domain provisions vhost + document root via the engine. Engine access is required.
server_setup.mysql_hint: With MYSQL_PROVISION_ENABLED=true and valid credentials the panel runs real CREATE DATABASE. If off, only panel records are created.
server_setup.pg_hint: Same idea with POSTGRES_PROVISION_ENABLED and pdo_pgsql.
server_setup.wp_hint: Only WordPress is fully automated: MySQL database + domain + App installer.
server_setup.wp_step_engine: Engine running and API key correct
server_setup.wp_step_mysql: MySQL provisioning on and a MySQL DB exists for the user
server_setup.wp_step_installer: Use App installer: pick domain + database
server_setup.email_mirror_hint: Mailboxes are mirrored in panel/engine files today; real Postfix/Dovecot is a separate roadmap phase.

license.subtitle: License key and engine validation
license.engine_skip: No local key; engine call skipped
license.env_hint: Set LICENSE_KEY in .env on production; do not store keys in the browser.
license.try_key_hint: For testing only; keep production keys on the server.

dashboard.detail_cpu_intro: CPU summary and top processes by usage.
dashboard.detail_ram_intro: RAM and swap summary; top memory consumers.
dashboard.detail_disk_intro: Panel disk summary and fullest mount points.
dashboard.top3_disk_hint: Based on system mount points; panel root may differ.
dashboard.system_admin_only: Server resource summary is shown to admins via the engine only.
dashboard.system_engine_hint: If the engine is down, stats will not appear. Check the engine and ENGINE_INTERNAL_KEY.

databases.title: Database Management
databases.name: Database Name
databases.ui_url_missing: Web UI URL is not configured on the server:
databases.no_web_ui_for_type: No web UI is configured for this database type.
databases.grant_host_hint: localhost = same server; % = any host (use carefully)
databases.password_rotated: Password rotated — save the new password

common.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this?

files.subtitle: Full path, drag-and-drop, double-click edit, and content search
files.name: Name
files.drop_zone_hint: Drag files onto this list or use «Upload file» above for multi-select.
files.quick_new_file: New file (with extension, e.g. index.html) — opens in the editor when editable
files.invalid_filename: Invalid name: use letters, digits, dot, hyphen, underscore only; no path separators.
files.file_exists: A file with this name already exists.
files.unsaved_close: You have unsaved changes. Close anyway?
files.cannot_edit: This file type cannot be opened in the editor

backups.subtitle: Full / files / database backups
backups.restore_unavailable: No engine backup id for this row (take a new backup)

cron.subtitle: Presets, custom cron expression, and quota summary
cron.info_fields: Format: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week — five fields (same order as Linux crontab).
cron.info_timezone: Times use the panel app timezone: {{tz}} (align with your server/engine).
cron.info_engine: Jobs are stored via the engine; actual execution may still require a server-side cron runner.
cron.info_paths: Use absolute paths in commands (php, wget, your site directory).
cron.examples_body:
# Laravel (change path to your site)
0 * * * *  →  /usr/bin/php /var/www/site/public_html/artisan schedule:run

# WordPress ping
*/15 * * * *  →  wget -q -O - https://example.com/wp-cron.php?doing_wp_cron

# PHP script
0 2 * * *  →  /usr/bin/php /var/www/site/public_html/scripts/backup.php
cron.empty_hint: No tasks yet. Add one above or check your package quota.
cron.custom_fields_hint: The fields below keep the single line in sync.

tools.title: Site tools
tools.subtitle: Composer / npm in the site document root (engine: manage_site_tools)

system.title: System
system.subtitle: Server resources, services, and web server
